name: Sizzling Sichuan Scallops
recipeIngredient: 3 tbsp Peanut or corn oil (or more if needed)
1 Cucumber, peeled and cut into 1/2-inch thick pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Pat the scallops dry. In a bowl, toss scallops with the marinade; set aside for 30 minutes.
2. In another bowl, mix together the chicken stock, soy sauce, rice wine, sugar, and cornstarch; set aside.
3. Preheat the wok over medium-high heat. Add 2 tablespoons of oil and half the salt. Add dry scallops to the hot oil and stir-fry for about 1 minute.
4. When the scallops begin to feel firm to the touch, remove from the wok; set aside.
5. Reheat the wok. Add 1 tablespoon of oil. Add ginger, garlic, remaining salt, green onions, and chili paste; cook gently for 15 seconds.
6. Turn heat to high. Toss in cucumbers. Stir-fry for 30 seconds.
7. Add the sauce mixture and stir quickly to thicken. Return the scallops to the wok, add vinegar and sesame oil; quickly toss for a few seconds to reheat.
8. Place the cast iron platter on a burner over high heat for 5 to 10 minutes or place in a very hot oven. Sprinkle a few drops of water to test the heat. It should bubble and sizzle.
9. Carefully place the hot, sizzling platter on top of a heatproof trivet. As soon as the scallops are finished, transfer them to a plate and quickly pour the mixture onto the hot platter, listening to it sizzle.
10. Serve hot. Makes 4 servings as the main entree or up to 8 as part of a multicourse Chinese menu.
